title: Boat Rental in Trapani: A Complete Guide for 2026
html:
To explore the Egadi Islands and the salt flats from the water, renting a boat in Trapani is the finest way to spend a day. With prices starting around €200 for a small motorboat, you can discover hidden coves and historic ports. BoatHire24 connects you with verified owners for a seamless booking experience.

Where to set off in Trapani

Your journey begins at one of several excellent departure points. Porto di Trapani is the main harbour, offering easy access to the open sea. For a more exclusive start, consider Marina di Trapani, which provides full services and parking. Other popular spots include the small port at Marausa Lido and the historic fishing harbour of Saline Nubia, both ideal for exploring the Stagnone Nature Reserve. From each you can reach the Egadi Islands, Favignana, Levanzo, and Marettimo, in 20 to 40 minutes.

Which boat to rent

BoatHire24 lists a variety of vessel types suitable for different trips. Check the Maiora 26m listing for an unforgettable luxury experience.

  1. Motorboat (6, 8 guests) Best for day trips around Favignana; from €200 half-day.
  2. Catamaran (10, 12 guests) Ideal for a stable cruise to Levanzo; from €800 full-day.
  3. RIB (4, 6 guests) Perfect for speed and access to sea caves; from €150 half-day.
  4. Sailboat (6, 8 guests) Great for a leisurely exploration of Marettimo; from €350 full-day.
  5. Luxury yacht (8, 12 guests) The Maiora 26m offers crewed charters with premium amenities; from €3,500 day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Trapani?

Rental prices vary by boat type and season, ranging from €50 per person for shared tours to €3,500 per day for a crewed yacht. For a cost-effective option, consider a shared tour of the Egadi Islands, which starts at around €50 per person for a half-day. A small motorboat can be hired from €150 for a half-day excursion. Catamarans typically cost from €800 for a full-day rental. Crewed yachts, including the Maiora 26m, command premium rates from €3,500 per day. You can browse boats for your dates to see real-time availability and prices.

  • Shared tour: from €50 per person (half-day)
  • Small motorboat half-day: from €150 (fuel not included)
  • Catamaran full-day: from €800 (up to 12 guests)
  • Crewed yacht: from €3,500 per day (luxury experience)

When is the best time to rent a boat in Trapani?

The peak season runs from June to September, when the weather is warm and the Mistral wind can bring choppy seas. Spring (April, May) and autumn (October) offer milder conditions and fewer crowds. Always check local forecasts before heading out.

Local tip: book early for July and August as boats are in high demand; consider a morning departure to avoid the strongest Mistral winds.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Trapani?

If you rent a boat with an engine power over 40 hp, Italian law generally requires a boating licence (patente nautica), but many rental operators include a skipper who holds the licence. For smaller boats (under 40 hp), no licence is needed, but operators may require a short briefing. Always confirm the exact requirements with the owner on BoatHire24. The platform’s the booking FAQ can answer common questions.

At a glance

  • Price band: €50, €3,500 per day
  • Peak months: June, September
  • Trip length: half-day (4 hours) or full-day (8 hours)
  • Guest capacity: 4, 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Trapani

Can I rent a boat without a skipper in Trapani?

Yes, you can rent small motorboats (under 40 hp) without a skipper if you have experience, but a licence is required for larger vessels.

What is the best island to visit from Trapani by boat?

Favignana, with its turquoise bays and the famous Cala Rossa, is the most popular destination, just a 20-minute crossing from Trapani.

How far is the Egadi Islands from Trapani?

The nearest island, Favignana, is about 7 nautical miles from Trapani harbour, a short motorboat ride of 20, 30 minutes.

Is it safe to rent a boat in Trapani for first-timers?

Yes, many operators offer a safety briefing and suggest calm-weather routes; hiring a skipper is recommended for those new to boating.

Whether you are a seasoned boater or a first-time renter, Trapani offers an unforgettable day on the water. Visit check availability and find your ideal vessel. If you own a boat in Trapani, consider listing it on BoatHire24 to reach thousands of travellers. BoatHire24 provides a trusted platform for both renters and owners to connect.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Trapani and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
